LAW AND MOTION 302, CROSS DEFENDANT AMERICAN EXPRESS CENTURION BANK DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT. NO APPEARANCES ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO CLRA ASPECT OF THE CAUSE OF ACTION UNDER BERRY VS. AMERICAN EXPRESS PUBLISHING, INC. (2007) 147 CAL.APP.4TH 224, 227. BODY OF CROSS-COMPLAINT REFLECTS THAT SUIT PERTAINS TO EXTENSION OF CREDIT. DEMURRER SUSTAINED WITH TEN DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO REMAINING ALLEGATIONS IN THE CAUSE OF ACTION. CROSS-COMPLAINANT TO PLEAD CAUSE(S) OF ACTION IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC RULE 2.112, SEPARATELY STATING EACH CAUSE OF ACTION. (MOVING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD; COURT REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Mar-15-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: CROSS DEFENDANT AMERICAN EXPRESS CENTURION BANK DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT. No appearances on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Demurrer is sustained without leave to amend as to CLRA aspect of the cause of action under Berry vs. American Express Publishing, Inc. (2007) 147 Cal.App.4th 224, 227. Body of cross-complaint reflects that suit pertains to extension of credit. Demurrer sustained with ten days leave to amend as to remaining allegations in the cause of action. Cross-complainant to plead cause(s) of action in compliance with CRC Rule 2.112, separately stating each cause of action. (Moving party to prepare a form of order.) (302/CWW) Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Kent Gubbine, CSR #5797
